clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Classify hypertensive disorders in pregnancy for diagnosis, risk stratification, and management routing
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Prenatal visits, triage, labor & delivery, postpartum assessment with elevated BP
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Disorder classification (Chronic HTN / Gestational HTN / Preeclampsia / Superimposed Preeclampsia) + severity determination

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for pregnancy BP assessment
Determine weeks of gestation (<20 vs ≥20 weeks)
Ask about HTN diagnosis before pregnancy or prior to 20 weeks
Screen for neurologic, hepatic, renal, hematologic, pulmonary findings
Red alert for SBP≥160 or DBP≥110

validationRules: If gestationalWeeks >= 20 and SBP/DBP meets hypertension threshold → further classification required

rules: First check if SBP ≥140 or DBP ≥90 → hypertension present
If no hypertension → normal, no disorder
If hypertension present and prior diagnosis OR <20 weeks → Chronic Hypertension
If hypertension present at ≥20 weeks, no proteinuria, no end-organ dysfunction → Gestational Hypertension
If hypertension at ≥20 weeks WITH proteinuria OR end-organ dysfunction → Preeclampsia
If prior chronic hypertension + new-onset proteinuria or end-organ dysfunction after 20 weeks → Preeclampsia Superimposed on Chronic Hypertension
If SBP ≥160 OR DBP ≥110 → flag as severe-range hypertension regardless of classification

category.description: e.g., 'Preeclampsia', 'Gestational Hypertension', 'Chronic Hypertension', 'Preeclampsia Superimposed on Chronic Hypertension'

endOrganDysfunction.description: List of affected systems if present

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Immediate maternal-fetal medicine consult + consider delivery
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Urgent BP management + fetal assessment + close monitoring

appDisplay.behavior: Do not proceed to routine follow-up; immediate escalation required

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Maternal-fetal medicine or OB emergency consultation
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Severe-range: immediate; Gestational HTN: repeat in 1 week; Chronic HTN: routine prenatal care
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Verify severe-range BP with repeat in 15 minutes
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Page OB provider immediately for severe-range or preeclampsia
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log disorder type, severity, proteinuria status, end-organ findings to prenatal record
